text.title: Newsletter Growth Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Newsletter Growth application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: The newsletter MUST be positionable in one sentence naming audience + outcome + frequency. Without it, growth tactics are rejected.
rule.rationale: Unfocused newsletters churn faster than they grow; positioning is the prerequisite, not an optimisation.

rule.statement: Signup form MUST be paired with a concrete lead magnet (asset / template / cheatsheet). Email-only signup is rejected for tier 1 growth.
rule.rationale: Bare email forms convert at 1-2%; lead-magnet forms convert at 5-15%.

rule.statement: Primary signup form MUST be above the fold on every newsletter-relevant page (homepage + posts). Footer-only signup is rejected.
rule.rationale: Above-fold placement triples capture rate vs footer-only on identical traffic.

rule.statement: Signup MUST use double opt-in. Single opt-in is rejected; complaint risk is too high.
rule.rationale: Double opt-in protects deliverability and produces a more engaged baseline.

rule.statement: Plan MUST include one referral mechanism (built-in referral program OR share-to-unlock OR forwarding ask). Pure paid acquisition is rejected as the only growth lever.
rule.rationale: Compounding referral beats paid acquisition on solo budgets.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
